headline: GOLD RETREATS TO $4,592 AFTER SHEDDING $50; STILL UP $1,351 FROM ONE YEAR AGO
snippet: Gold stood at $4,592/oz as of May 1, pulling back $50 from the prior session as the dollar steadied. The metal has corrected roughly 18% from its January peak above $5,600 but remains $1,351 higher year-on-year, underpinned by persistent central bank buying — roughly 755 tonnes expected in 2026 — and elevated geopolitical risk from the Middle East.

headline: SILVER CRACKS $76 AS DOLLAR SINKS TO TWO-MONTH LOW; CHINA IMPORTED RECORD 836 TONNES IN MARCH
snippet: Silver climbed above $76/oz on May 1, extending a multi-session rally as the U.S. dollar index fell to a two-month low on suspected Japanese yen intervention. China's silver imports hit a record 836 tonnes in March, driven by solar panel manufacturing and retail investment demand — reinforcing the structural supply-demand bull case for silver.

headline: COPPER HOLDS $5.93/LB ON CHINA MANUFACTURING BEAT; CHILE REFINERY SUPPLY CHAIN FRAYING
snippet: Copper futures steadied at $5.93/lb on May 1 after five consecutive losing sessions, drawing support from stronger-than-expected Chinese manufacturing data. Chile's refining operations face growing supply risk as the Middle East conflict cut sulphur shipments — a critical input for roughly half of Chile's copper processing — while Grasberg in Indonesia continues running below capacity after a fatal mudslide triggered a force majeure.

headline: GOLDSEEK COT REPORT MAY 1: GOLD, SILVER AND USDX SPECULATOR POSITIONING WEEKLY UPDATE
snippet: GoldSeek's weekly Commitments of Traders analysis covers speculative net longs and commercial hedger positions in gold, silver, and the U.S. dollar index as of May 1. Essential weekly reading for traders watching whether speculator positioning has cleared enough to support the next leg higher after precious metals corrected from their January highs above $5,600.

headline: RARE EARTHS WEEK: $2.8B SERRA VERDE DEAL, CHINA QUIETLY TIGHTENS SUPPLY — APRIL 27 TO MAY 1
snippet: The week ending May 1 saw major rare earth consolidation: USA Rare Earth's $2.8B acquisition of Brazil's Serra Verde mine, Critical Metals Corp's $835M bid for European Lithium's Tanbreez heavy rare earth deposit in Greenland, and a new Rare Earths Americas IPO. China Northern Rare Earth raised Q2 2026 prices as domestic inventories sit at historically low levels and processing faces sulphuric acid shortages.

headline: EMPEROR METALS DRILLS 1.46MOZ GOLD RESOURCE IN QUEBEC WITH VISIBLE GOLD IN CORE — DUQUESNE WEST
snippet: Emperor Metals upgraded its Duquesne West gold project in Quebec to a 1.46 million ounce resource estimate, backed by 23,000 metres of drilling with fresh core intercepts showing visible gold. Published April 28, this is one of the standout junior gold discoveries of the 2026 Quebec exploration season and a company worth adding to the watch list.

headline: SPANISH MOUNTAIN GOLD CLOSES $22.5M FIRST TRANCHE OF $55M WHEATON PRECIOUS METALS ROYALTY
snippet: Spanish Mountain Gold closed the first $22.5M tranche of a $55M royalty sale to Wheaton Precious Metals, granting a 1.5% NSR royalty on gold and silver production from its British Columbia project. The phased deal — $12.5M more after 60,000m of drilling and $20M at construction approval — funds the path to a feasibility study targeting a 2028 build decision in BC's Cariboo Gold Corridor.

headline: NEXGEN EYES SUMMER 2026 CONSTRUCTION START ON ROOK I — CANADA'S BIGGEST URANIUM PROJECT
snippet: NexGen Energy plans to break ground this summer on Saskatchewan's Rook I uranium project after receiving CNSC construction approval in March, with a four-year build schedule and a first-year construction cost of CAD $300M. Anchored by the Arrow deposit, Rook I targets nearly 30 million lbs U3O8 per year — roughly 20% of current global supply and over 50% of western supply — making it one of the largest new uranium developments on earth.

headline: CORE LITHIUM FIRES UP FINNISS MINE IN MAY WITH GLENCORE BACKING; TARGETS Q3 SPODUMENE CONCENTRATE
snippet: Core Lithium restarted mining operations at its Finniss project in Australia's Northern Territory this month, backed by a A$290M funding package anchored by Glencore, after halting in 2024 during the lithium price collapse. The restart shifts from open-pit to underground mining at the BP33 deposit, targeting first spodumene concentrate in Q3 2026 — a direct bet on a sustained lithium price recovery.

headline: CONTRARIAN CALL: GOLD MINERS DOWN 25% FROM JANUARY HIGHS — MAY NEED TIME TO CONSOLIDATE
snippet: With GDX and GDXJ off roughly 25% from January peaks — when gold briefly traded above $5,600 — one analyst argues the mining sector needs a consolidation phase before the next leg higher. The thesis: record margins at majors like Newmont and Agnico Eagle may be peaking as capital expenditure programmes shrink. A useful counterpoint before bottom-fishing the dip in juniors.

headline: HAYWOOD: GOLD MINERS STILL AT 10% EBITDA DISCOUNT TO HISTORY — 'SETUP RESEMBLES PRIOR BULL MARKETS'
snippet: Haywood Securities argues gold equities remain structurally cheap after the correction, trading at a 10% discount on EV/EBITDA and 1.5x P/NAV versus historical averages, while gold producers are printing record margins. The broker forecasts gold averaging $4,906/oz in 2026 and singles out Equinox Gold as a top pick, targeting 500,000+ oz annually from its twin Canadian Valentine and Greenstone mines.
